title: organization — helper functions
description: Two helpers. `active_organization()` returns the currently-resolved Organization (from the `organization.resolve_active` middleware). `default_organization()` returns the tenant's is_default row. Both are cached per-request. NO getter equivalent for descendants / ancestors — callers use the trait accessors on the model directly.

helpers.purpose: The Organization resolved for the current request by the `organization.resolve_active` middleware. Reads: (1) `X-Organization-Id` header (ULID or slug), (2) `me.default_organization_id` (user's pinned org), (3) tenant.default_organization (via cache).
behaviours: Returns null on unauthenticated + platform-admin requests (no organization context).
Returns null when the resolved id does not exist / is soft-deleted (middleware would have already 404ed the request in that case).
The resolution is cached on the RequestContext singleton so multiple calls in one request cost O(1).
helpers.flow: 1) Read RequestContext::organization() if set. 2) Fall through to middleware resolution. 3) Return null when unresolved.

helpers.purpose: The tenant's is_default=true row. Cached in Redis at `org:default:{tenant_id}` for 15 minutes.
behaviours: Explicit $tenantId → bypasses request context, useful in queue workers.
Returns null when the tenant has no is_default=true row — a state that should NEVER occur post-provisioning + is a critical alert (see health probes).

helpers.purpose: Convenience helper that returns an Eloquent Builder scoped to the current tenant's Organizations. Composed with the tenant scope so callers never re-add tenant_id manually.
behaviours: organization_scope()->tree() → adds the ORDER BY tree_path clause
Always applies status filter EXCLUDING archived; callers include archived via ->withArchived().

examples.title: Read the active org in a controller
Iterate descendants of the active org
Fetch the tenant's default from a queue worker
Build a tree view
